rulings.Case Title: SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES VS. UNION BANK, N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 3, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES' Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Plaintiff's motion is timely. Although Plaintiff's motion is styled as a motion to compel responses to discovery, the motion seeks production of emails that Defendant's discovery responses asserted had been produced, and is properly classified as a motion under C.C.P. Section 2031.320(a). There is no 45-day deadline to bring such a motion as there is with a motion under C.C.P. Section 2031.310. Plaintiff's Special Interrogatory No. 74 of Plaintiff's Set 4 specially prepared interrogatories asked for identification of all documents including e-mails regarding Sylvia Omoto's work on the project that led to the reclassification of the Customer Service Manager Position. Defendant responded to Interrogatory 74 that it had produced the documents. Plaintiff's Request for Production Set No. 5 asked Defendant to produce all documents identified in response to plaintiff's special interrogatories Set 4. Defendant shall produce Ms. Omoto's emails with members of the task force members regarding assigned percentages of time for tasks and draft descriptions for the CSM position after reclassification. Both parties' request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Compel Disco
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 3, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 3, PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S (A PSEUDONYM) MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Compel DiscoVERY. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: CONTINUED TO JUNE 12, 2013 AT 9:00 A.M. Plaintiff set a hearing for June 3rd, but served her motion on May 17th, fewer than the 16 court-days prior to the hearing required by CCP Section 1005(b). Defendant may file a new opposition, if defendant wishes to do so, and plaintiff may file a reply to any new opposition. Any new opposition shall be filed no later than nine court days prior to the continued hearing, and any reply at least five court days before the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 3, 2013, at 9:30 am, line 16, PLAINTIFF SHAWN REFOUA's DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. The matter is continued to June 17, 2013 per the agreement of the parties. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF EMMANUEL C. MONTOYA VS. SAI-SUN S. HO MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 3, 2013, at 9:30 am, line 17, DEFENDANT SAI-SUN HO's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion is continued to July 9, 2013, to give the opposing party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Please provide courtesy copies of the opposition with Department 302 by June 10, 2013. Please attach a cover letter with the courtesy copies reflecting the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
